calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-12-2014 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-16-2013 Set For Trial On May-27-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARCELL PATRICK NERI OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. (CCP 437c.) No opposition filed. Defendant CPMC has demonstrated that it did not breach any duty to Ms. Gebremedhin. (UMF nos. 7 - 10.) " ' "The standard of care against which the acts of a physician are to be measured is a matter peculiarly within the knowledge of experts; it presents the basic issue in a malpractice action and can only be proved by their testimony [citations], unless the conduct required by the particular circumstances is within the common knowledge of the layman." [Citations.]' [Citations.]" (Flowers v. Torrance Mem'l Hosp. Med. Ctr. (1994) 8 Cal.4th 992, 1001.) Defendant CPMC has demonstrated that, to a reasonable medical probability, the care and treatment provided by Defendant CPMC did not cause any injury to Ms. Gebremedhin. (UMF no. 11.) "The law is well settled that in a personal injury action causation must be proven within a reasonable medical probability based [on] competent expert testimony. Mere possibility alone is insufficient to establish a prima facie case." (Jennings v. Palomar Pomerado Health Systems, Inc. (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 1108, 1118.) Mr. Hadera cannot prevail on his loss of consortium claim that is contingent on Ms. Bebremedhin's claims. "[A]n unsuccessful personal injury suit by the physically injured spouse acts as an estoppel that bars the spouse who would claim damages for loss of consortium." (Meighan v. Shore (1995) 34 Cal.App.4th 1025, 1034-35. See UMF no. 15) ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR # 12998 =302/MJM
